name: Mililani Mauka
desc: Newer uphill extension — cooler mountain air, newer homes, slightly more upscale, Waianae Mountain views.
insider: The elevation difference from Mililani Town is noticeable — it's genuinely 5-10 degrees cooler up here, especially at night
The Mililani Mauka community pool and recreation center are newer and less crowded than Mililani Town's facilities
Kipapa Trail access is nearby for solid ridge hiking without the long drive to the North Shore or Windward side
HOA is strict but keeps the neighborhood immaculate — read the CC&Rs before buying because they regulate everything from paint colors to yard ornaments
The Sunday farmers market at Mililani High School parking lot has excellent local produce and prepared food vendors
Homes up here were mostly built in the 2000s-2010s — modern construction, better insulation, and hurricane-rated standards
Waianae Mountain views from the western-facing homes are spectacular at sunset — the Koolau views from the east side aren't bad either
The walking paths connecting Mauka neighborhoods to Mililani Town are well-paved and shaded — excellent for family bike rides
Central Oahu Regional Park (CORP) is right below with baseball diamonds, tennis courts, and a running track — the facilities are top-notch
Mililani Mauka consistently ranks among the safest communities on Oahu — HPD reports minimal property crime up here
